verdicts.interest_take: Resolves the season's sign mystery and stages the gift app's own emblem being destroyed; strong 'never caught the whole story' beat.
verdicts.writing_notes: title/moment/idea/why all distinct; idea is the real payoff (refusal, not just whodunit); warm, concrete, no dialogue.

verdicts.interest_take: The 'Oklahoma' honesty code paying off across two seasons is a true 'I never caught that' callback.
verdicts.writing_notes: Clean four-part structure; idea names the S1E5 origin, why lands the emotional double meaning; warm.

verdicts.interest_take: A throwaway gag that hides a genuine Polish folk tradition; classic pause-and-discover.
verdicts.writing_notes: idea delivers the real tradition payoff; transcript-spelling caveat handled gracefully; right category.

verdicts.interest_take: The actor who plays Roy Kent wrote this episode, and self-taped his way into the role; surprising dual credit.
verdicts.writing_notes: moment flags it as production note; idea carries the audition twist; concise and warm.

verdicts.interest_take: The rare character who gives Ted unvarnished truth instead of reassurance; a clear-eyed relationship beat.
verdicts.writing_notes: Distinct from the other relationship cards; idea is the payoff (truth-teller), no dialogue.

verdicts.interest_take: Easy to miss as a real, beloved English tabletop game rather than a generic prop.
verdicts.writing_notes: idea supplies the history payoff; characterful; right category and tone.

verdicts.interest_take: Names the season's self-authorship theme where it gets stated out loud; modest surprise but real throughline.
verdicts.writing_notes: Honest about being an interpretive gloss, not an attribution; the stronger of the two naming-speech cards.

verdicts.interest_take: A nameable, enjoyable needle-drop that plays against how the match turns out; adds Aesthetics variety.
verdicts.writing_notes: Minor but tidy; idea/why distinct; keeps the set's category spread.

verdicts.interest_take: The ethic it states is already carried, concretely, by the BELIEVE-sign card; same USB-drive moment.
verdicts.writing_notes: Well written but duplicative; keeping both means two cards on one beat. Cut to tighten.

verdicts.interest_take: The Van Damme rebrand is the lighter payoff of the same Zava naming speech already covered by the philosophy card.
verdicts.writing_notes: Reads as recap; duplicates the naming-speech beat and leans on a season-long forward thread.

verdicts.interest_take: Mostly plot recap; only lands if you already know the season's streak and recovery.
verdicts.writing_notes: Lowest-interest plot beat; requires forward knowledge to matter. Cut as recap.

episode_notes: Strong, content-rich episode but the reviewed set carried two duplicate pairs and a recap card. Pair 1: the BELIEVE-sign reveal vs 'Ted won't coach on contempt' both turn on the same USB-drive moment; kept the sign reveal (more concrete, resolves the mystery, and is the app's emblem) and cut the abstract ethics restatement. Pair 2: Zava's self-naming philosophy vs the keeper's Van Damme rebrand are the same speech; kept the philosophy card (states the idea, self-contained) and cut the lighter payoff (which also leans on a season-long thread). Also cut 's3e4-richmond-lose-streak-begins' as forward-dependent plot recap. Excluded candidates (pressure-theme, rebecca-rupert-proxy-war) were correctly held: broad and low-surprise, no rescue. Final 8 have good variety: ethics/emblem, two relationships, two real-world references/easter-eggs, one philosophy, one music cue, one production note. Best-first ordering leads with the emblem reveal and the Oklahoma callback (the two warmest 'never caught that' beats), with the lighter aesthetics/production cards trailing.
